title: Check If Digits Are Equal in String After Operations II
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C proposal for this challenge: the minimum-memory champion. Recommended and speed proposals are in C++ (see crossReferences).

variants.languageReason: C has the smallest runtime/stdlib baseline among the allowed languages and works directly with char* input; Lucas/CRT avoids dynamic allocation entirely.
variants.approachJustification: Compute each binomial coefficient modulo 2 and 5 with Lucas theorem/bit parity, combine modulo 10 via CRT, and accumulate the weighted adjacent-difference sum.

reasoningSummary: The final two digits are Pascal-row weighted sums. The memory proposal computes each coefficient modulo 10 from Lucas residues modulo 2 and 5, avoiding all dynamic allocation.
